findings.finding: Task 7 misses residual docstring and argparse prog= strings in Python modules. Verified: claude/pipeline/metrics.py:1026 prog="python3 -m claude.pipeline.metrics", claude/overnight/auth.py:322, claude/common.py:456/464/474 usage prints, claude/dashboard/data.py:286 Sphinx docstring, claude/overnight/batch_runner.py:3 :mod: role. just test does not fail on stale docstrings so the wheel ships with old names in --help output and tracebacks.
findings.reviewer_angle: sed-pattern correctness and false-negative verification gaps
findings.evidence_quote: Substitute any remaining `claude.<subpkg>` prose strings in docstrings or argparse metadata.

findings.finding: Verification grep extension scope narrower than 'rename every reference' claim. Neither Task 5 grep nor spec AC grep include *.yaml/*.yml/*.ps1 or extension-less executables (bin/audit-doc, bin/count-tokens, bin/validate-spec). No immediate runtime impact but guardrail not watertight for future additions.
findings.reviewer_angle: sed-pattern correctness and false-negative verification gaps

findings.finding: Ticket 128 (pre-commit hook rejecting main commits during overnight) is the exact guardrail that would make the atomic-land invariant enforceable against concurrent overnight sessions and agent branch-confusion, but status is backlog. .git/hooks/pre-commit does not exist. /commit skill has zero branch-awareness — never checks git branch --show-current. Plan does not declare 128 a prerequisite nor provide a compensating check.
findings.evidence_quote: Create the feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/114-cortex-cli-skeleton`.

findings.finding: Overnight runner derives target branch from session state (runner.sh:266-269: print(data.get('integration_branch') or 'main')), not from current HEAD. Task 1's one-shot overnight-pause covers only sessions running at branch-creation; a scheduled launchd/tmux session spawned during Tasks 2-14 picks up integration_branch='main' from its state JSON and does 'git worktree add' against whatever main's HEAD is at that moment.
findings.evidence_quote: Check `ps aux | grep -E 'overnight-(start|schedule|run)'` and `launchctl list | grep cortex` for active runners; stop them via the corresponding `overnight-*` stop command or `launchctl unload` before proceeding.

findings.finding: Task 5/7 miss filesystem-path literals referencing claude/ tree. justfile:625 hardcodes PID_FILE="claude/dashboard/.pid"; claude/overnight/runner.sh:68 sets PROMPT_TEMPLATE="$REPO_ROOT/claude/overnight/prompts/orchestrator-round.md". Neither matches python3 -m claude. nor uvicorn claude. nor from|import claude. patterns. After Task 2 git mv, app.py writes _pid_file to cortex_command/dashboard/.pid but justfile dashboard recipe reads claude/dashboard/.pid — 'already running' guard breaks silently.
findings.reviewer_angle: [build-system] + active .venv coupling and live overnight/launchd collision risk
findings.evidence_quote: `justfile` (5 lines: 615, 621, 655 uvicorn, 659, 663), `claude/overnight/runner.sh` (pre-rename; lives at `cortex_command/overnight/runner.sh` post Task 2) lines referencing `-m claude.*`

findings.finding: Task 14 brittleness: uses sorted(glob.glob('dist/cortex_command-*.whl'))[-1] without cleaning dist/ or bumping version. Synthesizer re-classified from A to B — hatchling rebuilds same-named wheel deterministically, sorted()[-1] picks the single extant file. Scenario requiring stale wheel + timestamp tie to fool data-file assertions is unlikely; hygiene is valid polish, not fix-invalidating.
findings.reviewer_angle: task ordering correctness and file-collision races

findings.finding: Task 13 test-critical dependency set [4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11] covers all pytest-breaking changes, but because orphan-prose gaps (from claude.X in .md prompts) don't break pytest collection or mock.patch resolution (prompts are data files), Task 13 passes green with orchestrator prompts broken. Three verification layers (per-task greps, full pytest, wheel+install) all pass while first overnight session post-merge fails.
findings.reviewer_angle: task ordering correctness and file-collision races
findings.evidence_quote: Run `just test; echo "exit=$?"` — pass if last line = `exit=0`.
